title: ByteWolf Plugin Health v0.1
description: A runtime snapshot of one registered plugin: where it is in its lifecycle and whether it is healthy. Like an observation, a health snapshot carries its own freshness (max_age_s from checked_at) so a consumer does not read a long-stopped reading as current. See docs/workstreams/plugin-sdk.md.

contract_version.description: Pins the meaning of every field below.

lifecycle_state.description: Where the plugin is in register -> start -> (reload) -> stop, or 'failed' if a transition threw. 'reloading' is transient and only reachable for a reloadable plugin.

health.description: The plugin's self-reported health at checked_at. 'unknown' is a legitimate answer when no probe has run; it is safe to state, a false 'ok' is not.

checked_at.description: RFC 3339, UTC, with an explicit offset. When this snapshot was taken; freshness is measured from here.

max_age_s.description: How long this snapshot stays usable after checked_at. Past it the health is stale and must not be treated as current.

detail.description: Free text for audit, e.g. the reason for 'failed'. Never parsed for dispatch.

then.description: A failed plugin cannot be reported healthy: 'ok' with lifecycle_state 'failed' is a contradiction the schema rejects.
